title: coreopsis nuecensoides
intro: Coreopsis nuecensoides, also known as New Mexican tickseed, is a flowering plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its yellow flowers and hairy stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Coreopsis nuecensoides include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and supporting immune function, as well as its potential to act as an antiviral and antifungal agent. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential interactions.
